title: anthoxanthum spicatum
intro: Anthoxanthum spicatum, also known as sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its sweet fragrance and delicate green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can aid in soothing digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. Consuming sweet grass has been reported to offer various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving oral health, and acting as a natural breath freshener. Sweet grass can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil for internal or topical use, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
